steps.title: Read the roots from the numerator
steps.content: Shortcut (Vieta's): The fraction equals zero only when its numerator equals zero. The numerator $(x-7)(x-9)$ is already factored, giving roots $x = 7$ and $x = 9$. The excluded value $x = 4$ is neither root, so both are valid.

steps.title: Apply sum of roots = -b/a
steps.content: Sum of roots: $7 + 9 = 16$. Equivalently by Vieta's, the numerator expands to $x^2 - 16x + 63$, so sum $= -(-16)/1 = 16$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
